notes: SHIP v1.14.238 — GEOMETRY TRUTH (work order Ship 2). Forge never renders a collision as clean geometry again, and never presents a guessed chassis height as truth. ⚠️SPEC CORRECTION: the work order said to replace 'the hardcoded return 8' in master_nodeHeightU. THERE IS NO HARDCODED return 8 — that function was ALREADY a table (in-name NRU parse → GPU-family fallback → silent `return 1` default). The real defect was the DEFAULT: every model the family test missed was silently sized 1U. On a real master that mis-sizes the non-GPU fleet — SN5600 and DELL-R760 are both 2U and both were being drawn 1U. (1) MASTER_U_TABLE is now the ONLY height authority and carries ONLY field-confirmed heights (H100/H200/A100/HGX/DGX = 8U per John's photo + SYS-821GE vendor spec; GB200/GB300/B200 NVL72 tray = 1U; leaf/spine/tor/mgmt-sw = 1U). New master_nodeHeightInfo() returns {u,known} and reports u:null for anything else — never a guess. ⚠️CONTAINMENT (deliberate deviation, load-bearing): master_nodeHeightU() KEEPS its numeric contract (unknown → 1U footprint). Returning null globally would have been DATA LOSS: mscope_buildRacksFromSnapshot writes elev.slots into a PERSISTED deployment record, so dropping unknown-height hosts from slots[] would have baked deployments permanently MISSING their switches and servers, across four surfaces (Rack Map, Master search, mscope seeder, deploy) that this ship's device pass does not cover. PROVEN by differential test: master_nodeHeightU old-vs-new is identical over 60 model/dns inputs — 0 differences. The 1U footprint is a placeholder, not a claim: master_rackToElevation stamps those slots hgtUnknown:true (additive field) and Forge refuses to draw them as clean geometry. Rack Map still renders them at the old guessed 1U — PRE-EXISTING, unchanged, flagged as the follow-up (John's call). (2) ONE COLLISION AUTHORITY — preflight_run's inline occ-map is extracted VERBATIM into pure phantom_rackGeometry(slots,totalU,label); preflight calls it, Forge calls it. REUSE, NOT REBUILD: a second detector that could disagree with the HARD NO-GO one violates 'one door per feature'. PROVEN behavior-identical: 400 random racks / 1522 findings / 0 mismatches vs the original loop, same messages, same order. It returns per-slot conflicts (BOTH sides of an overlap flagged, John's ruling — verified: an 8U node and a 1U node sharing U7 flag each other, a clean slot stays clean) plus overflow. ⚠️CAUGHT PRE-SHIP: my first cut had it SKIP hgtUnknown slots in the occ-map. That would have made a HARD NO-GO safety detector LOSE findings it catches today (mscope-seeded deployments carry hgtUnknown slots) — a tech could stack two devices in one U and preflight would call the plan clean. Removed: every device occupies AT LEAST 1U, so the placeholder span is a sound LOWER BOUND, not a guess — any overlap it finds is real. It under-detects honestly above the bottom U; it never invents a collision. (3) REAL OCCLUSION FIX in drawGuts — the trap a colour branch could not solve: trays filled in hosts[] order, so a colliding device was ALREADY INVISIBLE (the later one painted over it) and recolouring cannot bring back what was never drawn. Now clean trays paint first, flagged trays paint LAST (never buried), and COLLIDING trays SPLIT into half-width lanes so BOTH sides of an overlap are visible side by side. Flagged trays carry a hazard hatch + hard border + ⚠ on the name, clipped so they never bleed into the neighbouring lane. Red = geometry is WRONG; gold = height UNKNOWN. Every new constant is * S (GS=2.5 supersample); NO new texture, NO new material (the O(5) CanvasTexture budget is intact); nothing is ever drawn on the CoreWeave plate. (4) sl.conflict is its OWN field — deliberately NOT a third value of sl.status: statusOf() maps anything ≠ 'racked' to 'pending' and the row toggle would destroy it on the first tap. Conflict is geometry, status is verify — orthogonal. (5) FLAGS SURFACED: the verify table gets a per-row marker + the reason in field language (U-SPAN CONFLICT WITH <host> · SHARED U<n> / CHASSIS OVERRUNS THE RACK / MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN · <model> — NOT DRAWN TO SCALE / HOSTNAME-LOCATION MISMATCH); a red banner rides above the table when the elevation cannot be trusted; a gold callout NAMES every unknown-height device with its model and U — that list IS the worksheet for seeding the site profile. Rack chips and the herotag carry a ⚠N FLAGGED indicator. (6) CROSS-RACK STRAYS — a host NAMED for one cab but FILED in another (gpu-c1-001-01 in c1:002) renders where the MASTER places it and is flagged HOSTNAME/LOCATION MISMATCH; never silently re-homed (naming convention is a site-profile call John has not made). Detection only fires when the hostname encodes THIS row's prefix, so hostnames with no location in them (leaf-01, spine-a) cannot false-positive. Unknown-height devices stay DRAWN and TAPPABLE — dropping them into a tray outright would cost the tech the ability to mark real gear RACKED — but they are ALSO named in the callout, so John's 'lands in the position-unknown tray' checklist item is satisfied without a workflow regression. LEGACY: every new CSS rule is #forge3d-sheet-scoped (rd-only); no legacy selector touched; the only shared-house code (master_nodeHeightU, preflight) is proven behavior-identical — ?legacy=1 stays byte-identical-behavior. GATES: node --check ×4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS braces 3856/3856 balanced, CRLF preserved (48434 CRLF / 0 bare LF), three-stamp .237→.238, differential tests PASS. DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD STOP, John iPhone) — IMPORT A REAL MASTER FIRST, then: (1) CARRY-FORWARD from the .235/.236/.237 pass, never yet exercised on a populated master: real racks + real hostnames render in the aisle; RU-less hosts show the gold NOT PLACEABLE callout; RACKED/PENDING badges visibly distinct (teal vs slate). (2) Colliding spans wear visible flags WITH REASONS in the table AND in 3D — and BOTH colliding devices are visible (split lanes), neither erased. (3) Unknown-model devices: gold hatch in 3D, named in the gold callout with model + U, still tappable to RACKED. (4) A clean rack renders UNFLAGGED — no red, no gold, no ⚠ on its chip. (5) Cross-rack stray (if the master carries one) flags HOSTNAME-LOCATION MISMATCH and is NOT re-homed. (6) ?legacy=1 unchanged. OPEN FOR JOHN: (a) confirm heights for the unknown models the callout names (SN5600, DELL-R760, SN2201, patch panels, NVLink trays, storage) → they seed MASTER_U_TABLE next ship; (b) rule on whether Rack Map / Master search should also stop guessing 1U — that would change persisted deployment seeding, so it needs its own ship + verify. NEXT per brief: reconciliation (§3.3), then provenance strips house-wide (§3.1 — refactor deploy_forge_provenance into ONE global helper FIRST), then SINGLE MASTER INGESTION POINT (§3.2), then the FORGE Command card (§3.4).
